title: leucocoryne codehuensis
intro: Leucocoryne codehuensis, also known as the mountain daffodil or white fairy, is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. The plant contains compounds with medicinal properties, including alkaloids and saponins, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Leucocoryne codehuensis include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antitumor properties, making it a subject of interest for further research. Leucocoryne codehuensis is often prepared as a tincture or infusion for medicinal use, although more studies are needed to fully understand its effects and potential applications.
